rationale_top3: Entertainment facilities are exceptional with a heated pool complex featuring water slides, jumping pillow, pedal karts, and daily kids activities coordinated by beloved staff member Nick, creating a resort-style experience that keeps families entertained on-site. Cleanliness is outstanding with amenities consistently described as spotless and immaculate, including a five-star quality camp kitchen, with multiple cleaning staff personally praised for their exceptional work. Prime waterfront location offers direct beach access via back gate, coastal walking paths with beautiful water views, and Coles/Woolworths across the road, allowing families to walk everywhere without needing a car.
rationale_honourable: Premium pricing at $100+ per night for powered sites is considered justified by most families who appreciate the resort-quality facilities, professional service, and convenient location, though budget-conscious travelers may find it expensive compared to other parks.
water_fun: Heated pool, two water slides, resort-style lagoon.
kids_play: Jumping pillow, pedal karts, daily activities, kids disco.
pet_detail: Dog-friendly with $10 per night fee; no dedicated dog facilities mentioned in reviews.
best_for: Families with toddlers and young children who value pristine cleanliness, resort amenities, and the ability to walk to beaches and shops without driving.
executive_summary:
PARK: BIG4 Caloundra Waterfront Holiday Park
SCORE: 88/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-28
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 440

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (19/20) — Reviews consistently highlight the "spectacular" heated pool complex with water slides that keep kids entertained for hours, complemented by jumping pillow, pedal karts, daily activities coordinated by "amazing" staff member Nick, kids discos, bingo nights, and poolside restaurant service. Families describe it as "resort-style" with "everything you need" on-site.

Nature (16/20) — The waterfront location provides direct access to coastal walking paths and Bulcock Beach via a back gate, with reviews praising "stunning walks along the coast" and "beautiful water views." The park features well-maintained gardens with native vegetation and bird-watching opportunities, though it's more suburban than bushland with some road noise.

Cleanliness (15/15) — Overwhelmingly described as "the cleanest campground" with "spotless" and "immaculate" amenities that are "always sparkling clean." The camp kitchen is called "five-star restaurant quality" and multiple cleaning staff are named personally for their "exceptional" work. Reviews repeatedly state "best ever" and "cleanest park in 20 years."

Value (11/15) — Pricing is described as "premium" and "expensive" at $100+ per night for powered sites and $350+ for cabins, with several reviews noting it's "50% more expensive" than comparable parks. However, families who stay repeatedly say "you get what you pay for" and the resort-quality facilities, location, and service justify the cost for many.

Site Size (7/10) — Premium sites with artificial grass are "spacious" and "generous," but tent/camper sites are described as "TINY" and "cramped" with some vans "hanging over onto the road." Drive-through sites exist but can be "very tight" for larger rigs. Cabins are well-sized for families.

Sentiment (10/10) — Exceptional repeat visitation with families returning annually for 3-12+ years. Reviews use phrases like "can't wait to come back," "best park we've stayed in," "already rebooked," and "this is our go-to destination." Net Promoter Score appears extremely high with families actively recommending to friends.

Location (10/10) — Perfect urban waterfront position with Coles/Woolworths "literally across the road," beaches and coastal walks "right outside the back gate," and Caloundra CBD within "easy walking distance." Families praise being able to "walk everywhere" without needing a car, close to Australia Zoo, markets, and Sunshine Coast attractions.

WHY NOT 100:
Premium pricing puts the park out of reach for budget-conscious families, with multiple reviews noting the cost has increased significantly year-over-year and some loyal customers stating they "can't afford to stay here next time." Tent and camper sites along the back fence are small and experience constant traffic noise from the busy intersection, with reviews specifically warning against these sites. Limited dedicated kids playground equipment beyond the jumping pillow, with families noting the lack of traditional play structures for younger children who find the pillow too rough when older kids are present.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"Simply the best caravan park in Queensland," "spotless and exceptionally clean amenities," "resort style pool with water slides," "best camp kitchen we have ever seen," "walking distance to everything," "kids absolutely loved it," "staff go above and beyond," "premium price but worth every cent," "tent sites are TINY," "traffic noise constant until midnight"

rationale_top3: Exceptional animal experiences with daily guinea pig cuddles and deer feeding described as 'magical' and 'phenomenal', with Ranger Steve and family receiving extraordinary praise for creating engaging, educational interactions that keep children captivated throughout their stay. Beautiful hinterland setting with stunning mountain views, Mary River access for kayaking and platypus spotting, and proximity to Booloumba Creek swimming holes, all while maintaining a peaceful valley atmosphere perfect for disconnecting from daily life. Consistently praised cleanliness with facilities described as 'some of the cleanest camping grounds we've stayed at', combined with extremely generous, well-spaced sites that allow families to feel comfortable even when the park is fully booked.
rationale_honourable: Strong value perception among overnight guests who feel the comprehensive facilities and unique animal experiences justify costs, though pricing noted as higher-end; diverse entertainment options including Jurassic Trail, movie nights, disco events, and fairy garden create a complete experience that generates high repeat visitation with many families returning annually.
water_fun: No on-site pool; nearby creek swimming
kids_play: Daily animal feeding, guinea pig cuddles, movie nights
pet_detail: Dog friendly at most campsites (some accommodation not pet friendly)
best_for: This park is best for families with children aged 0-13 who prioritize hands-on animal experiences, nature-based activities, and a wholesome, safe camping environment over resort-style amenities.
executive_summary:
PARK: Kookaburra Park
SCORE: 87/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-25
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 136

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (19/20) — Exceptional animal experiences dominate reviews with daily guinea pig cuddles and deer feeding described as 'magical' and 'phenomenal'; Ranger Steve and family praised extensively for creating engaging, educational interactions; regular movie nights, disco events, Jurassic Trail hike, fairy garden, and basketball/volleyball areas provide diverse activities; multiple families report children 'never wanted to leave' and made friendships with ranger's children.

Nature (17/20) — Beautiful hinterland setting with 'stunning scenery everywhere' and mountain views; Mary River access for kayaking and platypus spotting; proximity to Booloumba Creek (10 min) for swimming; river walk and Jurassic Trail with spectacular lookout views; abundant wildlife including wallabies, kookaburras, and birdlife; described as 'peaceful valley surrounded by mountains' with fresh country air and dark skies for stargazing.

Cleanliness (14/15) — Facilities consistently praised as 'some of the cleanest camping grounds we've stayed at' with hot showers and clean toilets; camp kitchen and amenities 'very well kept' and 'impeccable'; grounds well-maintained with mowed sites and tidy trails; one older amenities block noted as needing replacement (scheduled for demolition); occasional mention of overgrown areas but overall property described as 'incredibly well maintained'.

Value (12/15) — Reviews note pricing is 'on the expensive side' with day visit costing $35 for family causing one negative review; most families feel facilities and experiences justify costs with 'reasonable and fair' fees reflecting service quality; free animal feeding appreciated; coffee van and shop on-site convenience valued; no major value complaints from overnight guests but not described as budget-friendly.

Site Size (9/10) — Sites consistently described as 'extremely generous', 'large', and 'well spaced out' so families 'don't feel on top of other campers'; multiple site options from powered to off-grid; specific sites like Pine Tree Hill, Flat Hill, and Damside praised for space and views; campers note 'plenty of space' even when fully booked; sites accommodate families comfortably with room for gazebos and outdoor activities.

Sentiment (9/10) — Overwhelmingly positive with families using superlatives like 'best camping experience ever', 'magical', and 'heaven'; extremely high repeat visitation with many annual returnees and multi-visit families; strong recommendations throughout with 'highly recommend' appearing in majority of reviews; staff particularly Steve/George/Oakley receive exceptional praise; handful of negative reviews regarding group bookings noise incident in 2023 and one day-visitor complaint prevent perfect score.

Location (7/10) — Kenilworth township 8-10 minutes away with famous bakery, dairy, and playground; Booloumba Creek 10 minutes for swimming; Little Yabba Creek 3-5 minutes; Noosa Beach approximately one hour away; Montville hinterland location provides peaceful setting but requires driving for most attractions; no mobile reception on-site (Vodafone) but available in town; not directly beach-accessible but well-positioned for hinterland exploration.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Value perception issues with pricing noted as 'expensive side' and one very negative day-visitor review regarding $35 fee for limited animal viewing without clear communication about feeding times
2. Maintenance inconsistencies with Jurassic Trail occasionally 'badly overgrown' and impassable, one older amenities block with blocked toilets/flooding issues (though scheduled for replacement), and some grounds noted as 'alittle over grown' with concerns about ticks
3. July 2023 group booking incident resulted in multiple 1-star reviews citing poor communication, immature management response via social media posts, and issues with noise complaint handling that damaged long-term customer relationships

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
'magical, a little piece of heaven on earth', 'Steve and Oakley are the most incredible duo', 'animal feeding was definitely my kids favorite', 'I swear this place cures burnout', 'some of the cleanest camping grounds we've stayed at', 'wholesome family location that is unique and perfect', 'We go back every year now', 'the best 3 nights away we've had in a long time'

park_name: Alex Beach Cabins and Tourist Park
rationale_top3: Consistently praised for immaculate cleanliness across cabins, amenities, and grounds with "spotlessly clean" facilities and excellent water pressure in showers that reviewers frequently cite as a standout feature Exceptional central Sunshine Coast location with 3-5 minute walk to beautiful patrolled Alex Beach, adjacent scenic lake with birdlife, and walking distance to cafes, restaurants, surf club and shopping while remaining peaceful and quiet Spacious powered sites with privacy hedges on level concrete slabs, heated dual pools with shade, playground, and popular regular food trucks (wood-fired pizza Wednesday/Saturday, daily coffee van) creating a relaxed family-friendly atmosphere with outstanding helpful staff
rationale_honourable: High repeat visitation with families returning year after year demonstrates genuine affection for the park, complemented by excellent value for money and beautiful natural surroundings including established palm trees creating a tropical feel and adjacent lake with walking/cycling paths for wildlife viewing
water_fun: Heated pool (two connected pools) with shade
kids_play: Playground onsite, beach walk, adjacent duck pond
pet_detail: Dog friendly on caravan sites (not cabins), beach on-leash area nearby, lake walks pet friendly
best_for: Families with young children wanting a clean, central Sunshine Coast base with easy beach access, excellent amenities, and a relaxed atmosphere enhanced by food trucks and friendly staff
executive_summary:
PARK: Alex Beach Cabins and Tourist Park
SCORE: 82/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-24
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 252

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (14/20) — Reviews highlight a heated pool (two connected pools with shade), playground, games room, regular visiting food trucks (wood-fired pizza van especially popular Wednesday/Saturday), and coffee van daily. Some families note the pool is small and can get crowded, and entertainment is more low-key rather than extensive programmed activities or waterpark features.

Nature (18/20) — Outstanding beach access with 3-5 minute walk to beautiful, patrolled Alex Beach with dog-friendly areas. Adjacent to a scenic lake/lagoon with birdlife, ducks, and walking/cycling paths. Park features established palm trees creating a tropical feel. Families love the natural surroundings for morning walks and wildlife viewing.

Cleanliness (15/15) — Consistently described as "immaculate," "spotlessly clean," "meticulously maintained" across cabins, amenities, and grounds. Showers praised for great water pressure. Gardens manicured, bins serviced daily, amenities cleaned regularly. Reviewers frequently cite cleanliness as a standout feature.

Value (14/15) — Generally described as "great value," "reasonable prices," and "excellent value for money." Stay 7 pay 6 deal noted positively. Some guests felt pricing was on the higher side for cabin size or ensuite sites, but most agreed quality matched the cost. Central location adds value.

Site Size (9/10) — Powered sites described as "excellent sized," "spacious," "roomy" with privacy hedges between sites. Level concrete slabs accommodate large vans easily. Some mention sites are "not a heap of space" but adequate. Cabins noted as compact but functional, with some feeling cramped for 3-4 people.

Sentiment (10/10) — Overwhelmingly positive with extremely high repeat visitation. Phrases like "will definitely be back," "stay every year," "rebooked for next year," "15 years returning" dominate reviews. Families express genuine affection for the park. Very high recommendation rate despite minor issues.

Location (10/10) — Perfect central Sunshine Coast position. Walking distance (3-5 minutes) to Alex Beach, surf club, cafes, restaurants, convenience store. Short drive to Maroochydore Plaza shopping centre. Between Maroochydore and Mooloolaba. Set back from main roads for peace and quiet while maintaining convenience. Universally praised.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Entertainment facilities are limited to pool and playground with no major waterpark, jumping pillow, mini golf, or organized kids' activities that would achieve a higher entertainment score.
2. Several reviews mention specific maintenance issues including faulty appliances, holes in cabins allowing bugs, dated fixtures, broken fridge/door locks, and poor garden maintenance during one extended stay period.
3. A few negative service experiences reported including rude phone manner from one staff member, refusal to allow booking changes for weather/health, poor handling of a booking error, and alleged serious safety incident with inadequate response.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"spotlessly clean and the showers had great water pressure," "immaculately presented caravan park," "staff were very friendly and helpful," "walking distance to beach, cafes, restaurants," "heated pool," "excellent sized caravan sites," "great location, close to everything," "5 minute walk to the beach," "kids loved the pool and playground," "meticulously clean," "staff go above and beyond," "will definitely be back"

rationale_top3: Outstanding beachfront location positioned between calm Maroochy River inlet and patrolled surf beach, with shops, cafes, and restaurants across the road providing families everything within walking distance Exceptional water access offering the best of both worlds - crystal clear calm inlet waters perfect for young children to swim safely alongside patrolled ocean beach for older kids and adults Consistently spotless and impeccably clean amenities that maintain high standards, with many families noting these as the cleanest facilities they've experienced in caravan parks
rationale_honourable: Cotton Tree Holiday Park earns Silver recognition primarily for its outstanding beachfront location between the Maroochy River inlet and patrolled surf beach, with shops, cafes and restaurants just across the road. While families consistently praise the spotless amenities, beautiful water access for children, and the convenience of having everything within walking distance, the park loses points for cramped sites, inconsistent customer service, high pricing without pool facilities, and site allocation issues that frustrate many guests.
water_fun: River inlet and patrolled beach access only
kids_play: No pool or dedicated kids entertainment facilities
best_for: Families with young children who want safe, calm water access and walking distance to beaches, cafes and shops, and who don't require large sites or onsite pool facilities.
executive_summary:
PARK: Cotton Tree Holiday Park
SCORE: 71/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-14
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 246

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (8/20) — Reviews consistently note "no pool or other entertainment" and describe the park as having "not much to offer except a beach" with "no play grounds" or organized kids activities, though food trucks occasionally visit on weekends.

Nature (19/20) — Families rave about the "best of both worlds, access to the Maroochydore river and the ocean" with "calm inlet on one side, surf patrolled beach on the other" providing "crystal clear calm waterways great for kids" and "easy swim for the kids" with "beautiful beach sunrises."

Cleanliness (13/15) — Recent reviews frequently mention "amenities spotless," "very clean," "impeccably clean," and "cleanest caravan park toilet I ever been," though some peak period reports note toilets "beyond filth" and amenities "gross" when overcrowded with insufficient cleaning frequency.

Value (8/15) — Families repeatedly complain about pricing: "$76-$84 per night very expensive with no pool or other entertainment," "$80 a night is outrageous," and "overpriced" is a common theme, with many noting other parks offer pools and facilities for less money.

Site Size (5/10) — The most consistent complaint across reviews: "sites are really small & close together," "packed in like sardines," "crammed in tight," "way too small," "very tight sites," with numerous families struggling to fit vans and experiencing difficulty maneuvering into cramped spaces.

Sentiment (8/10) — Reviews are polarized: loyal repeat visitors praise it as "our favorite park" and "home away from home," while many recent visitors state "will never return" due to rigid management, site issues, and poor customer service, particularly since ownership changes.

Location (10/10) — Universal praise for location: "perfect location," "prime location," "amazing location right by the inlet," "walking distance to shops, cafes, surf club," "everything is close and handy," positioned directly between river and ocean with Cotton Tree village across the road.

WHY NOT 100:
Sites are severely undersized for modern caravans with families reporting 20+ minute struggles to squeeze into allocated spaces, cars unable to fit on sites, and vans extending into neighboring areas, creating safety concerns and stress. Customer service has deteriorated significantly under current management with multiple families reporting "incredibly rude, abusive, condescending" staff, inflexible policies even for medical emergencies or extreme weather, unanswered phones and emails, and unauthorized credit card charges. The park commands premium waterfront pricing ($76-84/night) but provides no pool, playground, or entertainment facilities that families expect at this price point, especially compared to similarly-priced Big4 parks with comprehensive amenities.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"Perfect location, you have everything at your fingertips," "Easy swim for the kids, calm waters," "Sites are really small & close together & completely jammed packed in," "Very expensive to say the least with no pool or other entertainment," "The amenities are spotless," "Staff are incredibly rude, abusive, condescending," "Packed in like sardines," "Best of both worlds, access to the Maroochydore river and the ocean"

rationale_top3: Exceptional beachfront location with patrolled beach access within 2 minutes from anywhere in the park, plus walking distance to shops, cafes, surf club, Coles, and restaurants across the road Consistently clean and well-maintained amenities described as 'super clean', 'spotless', and 'clean as a whistle' with regular servicing throughout the day Complete absence of on-site entertainment facilities (no pool, playground, kids activities, or games room) combined with premium pricing ($74-$155+ per night) that guests find excessive for basic amenities
rationale_honourable: Coolum Beach Holiday Park earns its Silver rating primarily through an exceptional beachfront location that puts families steps from patrolled swimming and within easy walking distance of shops, cafes, and the surf club. The park's clean, modern amenities and well-maintained grounds create a pleasant base, though the lack of on-site kids' entertainment, cramped site sizes requiring tractor assistance, premium pricing, and recurring customer service complaints prevent it from reaching Gold status.
water_fun: Beach access only, no pool facilities
kids_play: No on-site playground or kids entertainment
pet_detail: Dogs allowed in designated areas (approximately half the park), $5 per dog per night fee applies, dog-friendly beach access available
best_for: This park best suits beach-loving families with older children or couples who value premium location over facilities and don't mind paying top dollar for direct beach access and town proximity
executive_summary:
PARK: Coolum Beach Holiday Park
SCORE: 68/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-20
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 173

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (6/20) — Reviews consistently note there is "no pool or kids playground" and "absolutely none of that here - just the site & bathrooms and camp kitchen." Multiple families stated "it's not very kid friendly" with no evening activities, games rooms, or organized entertainment. The park offers only a camp kitchen and basic facilities, with one reviewer noting "no extra touches to make the park special."

Nature (18/20) — The park scores exceptionally well for beach access, with reviews praising "best beach and easy access," "right on the beach," "short stroll over the dune to the patrolled beach," and "2 minutes to the beach from anywhere in the park." Dog-friendly beach access is highlighted repeatedly. A nearby skate park adds minor value. The only limitation is lack of bushland or creek environments.

Cleanliness (13/15) — Amenities receive strong praise: "super clean," "spotless," "very clean and well maintained," "amenities always clean and serviced," and "clean as a whistle." However, some reports of "slab was filthy on arrival," occasional toilet light failures, and "amenities nice but could be cleaner" prevent a perfect score.

Value (8/15) — Pricing is a major complaint throughout reviews: "daylight robbery," "overpriced," "way overpriced," "most expensive park we have stayed at," with costs ranging $74-$155+ per night. Additional fees frustrate guests: "$10 to park your van with tractor," "$5 per dog per night," "$6 washing machine." Many note "nothing special about the caravan park other than location" and compare unfavorably to parks with pools and activities at lower prices.

Site Size (6/10) — Site size generates significant criticism: "sites are too small for large vans," "very tight," "cramped in like sardines," "sites a bit cramped," "some almost impossible to get into." Many require "tractor to park vans" with associated fees. However, some premium sites are described as "enormous" and "more than big enough," indicating variability.

Sentiment (8/10) — Mixed sentiment with loyal returners ("27 years," "10 years," "30 years") balanced by those who "won't be going back" due to price increases and service issues. Positive phrases include "would happily stay again," "will definitely be returning," but significant negative experiences with staff ("extremely rude," "abusive and intolerant") and value concerns dampen overall satisfaction.

Location (9/10) — Location is the park's standout feature: "best location," "perfect location," "excellent location," "handy to everything." Reviews praise proximity to "shops across the road," "Coles, Dan Murphys, pub, restaurants," "surf club," and "walking distance to everything." The only minor limitation is road noise on some boundary sites.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of on-site kids' entertainment, pools, playgrounds, or organized activities that families expect at premium-priced parks, with multiple reviews stating it's "not very kid friendly" and offers no facilities beyond basic amenities.
2. Site sizing and accessibility issues requiring paid tractor assistance ($10 fee) for many sites, with widespread complaints about cramped, difficult-to-access spaces that are "too small for large vans" and feel like "sardines in a tin can."
3. Persistent customer service problems with reception staff described as "very rude," "unfriendly," "abusive," "seriously intense," and "not interested in any customer service," combined with poor communication (unanswered calls, unreturned messages) and inflexible booking policies that frustrate guests during health emergencies and special circumstances.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"best beach and easy access to everything", "super clean amenities", "cramped in like sardines", "staff are very rude, unfriendly", "most expensive park we have stayed at", "no pool or kids playground", "sites are too small for large vans", "location location location"

rationale_top3: Exceptional cleanliness standards set this park apart, with reviews consistently describing it as having "the cleanest amenities in any caravan park" with spotless facilities maintained daily at 7am, earning full marks in this critical category. The peaceful rural setting amongst working strawberry fields with views of the Glass House Mountains provides a genuine nature-adjacent experience, perfect for travellers seeking tranquility and natural surroundings rather than commercial holiday park atmosphere. Hosts Fay and Peter receive overwhelming praise for their warm hospitality, local knowledge, and community-building efforts, creating a welcoming atmosphere that resonates particularly well with grey nomads and mature couples seeking personal connection.
rationale_honourable: Beerwah Caravan Park earns Bronze recognition primarily for its exceptional cleanliness standards, with reviews consistently praising it as having "the cleanest amenities in any caravan park" and "spotless" facilities that are maintained daily. The park's rural setting amongst strawberry fields provides a peaceful, nature-adjacent experience near the Glass House Mountains, and the hosts Fay and Peter receive overwhelming praise for their welcoming hospitality and local knowledge, creating a genuine community atmosphere that resonates strongly with grey nomads and couples seeking tranquility.
water_fun: No pool or water facilities available
kids_play: No playground or kids entertainment facilities
pet_detail: Pet friendly - dogs welcome at sites
best_for: Grey nomads, couples, and mature travellers who prioritize exceptional cleanliness, peaceful surroundings, and personal hospitality over family entertainment facilities.
executive_summary:
PARK: Beerwah Caravan Park
SCORE: 62/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-24
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 108

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (4/20) — Reviews explicitly note "no special offers for kids" and confirm absence of pool, playground, jumping pillow, or any on-site entertainment. One German review specifically warned there are no facilities for children. Multiple reviews describe it as quiet and relaxing but with zero kids' activities.
Nature (14/20) — Park situated amongst working strawberry fields with views of Glass House Mountains nearby. Reviews mention rural agricultural setting with birds, herb gardens, and peaceful natural surroundings. Close proximity to hiking in Glass House Mountains and natural attractions, though not directly bushland-adjacent.
Cleanliness (15/15) — Overwhelming consensus across 100+ reviews: "cleanest amenities in any caravan park," "spotlessly clean," "immaculate," cleaned daily at 7am. Facilities described as "impeccable" with consistent praise for toilet, shower, kitchen, and laundry cleanliness standards.
Value (11/15) — Mixed feedback on value. Most paid $50/night for powered sites with some noting "good value" and free night per week for longer stays. However, several reviews called it "overpriced" for the location and lack of facilities, with one comparing unfavorably to nearby Ingenia at $36/night.
Site Size (9/10) — Reviews consistently mention "spacious sites," "large sites" (10m x 13m), concrete pads, adequate space between vans, and easy accommodation of 31ft motorhomes. Power and water readily accessible without needing extension cords.
Sentiment (7/10) — Highly polarized. Majority love the cleanliness and hosts (Fay and Peter praised extensively), with many saying "will definitely stay again." However, concerning number of 1-star reviews citing rude management, confrontational interactions, and issues with permanent residents. Family travellers less enthusiastic.
Location (2/10) — Repeatedly described as "far from everything," "middle of nowhere," "a bit out of the way," 7km off highway. Main attraction is proximity to Australia Zoo (8-10 minutes drive). Town of Beerwah 5 minutes away for shops. Not near beaches or major family attractions beyond the zoo.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of family entertainment infrastructure (no pool, no playground, no kids' activities) makes it unsuitable for families with young children seeking holiday entertainment, losing all entertainment points.
2. Remote location "far from everything" with limited nearby family attractions beyond Australia Zoo, and 30+ minutes from beaches, significantly limiting location appeal for family holidaymakers.
3. Multiple concerning reviews about management being "rude," "bossy," and confrontational (shower time restrictions enforced abruptly, defensive behavior), plus issues with permanent residents speaking poorly to guests, creating inconsistent hospitality experience despite many positive reviews about Fay and Peter.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"Cleanest amenities in any caravan park I've seen", "no special offers for kids on this site", "close to Australia Zoo", "far from everything", "mostly permanent residents", "Fay and Pete are absolutely wonderful hosts", "very quiet and tranquil spot", "surrounded by strawberry fields", "spotlessly clean", "not the friendliest.. bit odd"

rationale_top3: Excellent location within 500 metres of Australia Zoo with courtesy shuttle bus service, plus central access to Sunshine Coast beaches and Glass House Mountains, making it ideal for families planning full-day zoo visits Strong natural bushland setting with wooded areas, lagoon/pond featuring ducks, turtles, eels and water dragons that children enjoy feeding, plus creek running behind some sites creating genuine nature immersion Spacious powered sites with generous room for larger vans and easy access, though unpowered sites are less consistent in quality
rationale_honourable: Mixed cleanliness standards with some praising clean amenities while others report mouldy showers and facilities needing deep cleaning and refurbishment, suggesting inconsistent maintenance across the park
water_fun: Small dated pool needing renovation, not inviting
kids_play: No playground area, limited kids entertainment facilities
pet_detail: Dog friendly park, pets welcome with accommodation
best_for: Families with young children who will spend days at Australia Zoo and want a quiet, natural setting with wildlife viewing at the pond rather than on-site entertainment
executive_summary:
PARK: Landsborough Holiday Park
SCORE: 58/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-21
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 123

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (8/20) — Reviews consistently note "no playground area for kids" and "pool super small" with multiple families stating "no catering whatsoever to kids." The park offers a firepit and BBQ area, but families with active children found limited entertainment options beyond feeding ducks at the pond. One family noted their kids "going stir crazy" with nowhere to play.

Nature (16/20) — Strong nature experience with "wooded area, felt nice to be in a natural surrounding" and a lagoon/pond that families loved. Multiple reviews mention "ducks, water birds, eels and water dragons" and "kids loved the ducks and turtles in the pond." The park features "lots of trees" and a "little creek running behind" some sites, providing genuine bushland atmosphere.

Cleanliness (11/15) — Mixed feedback with many praising "clean amenities" and "beautiful and clean" facilities, but several concerning reports including "mouldy in showers," "facilities were not clean," and "toilets could do with refurbishment and a deep clean." Recent reviews suggest cleanliness has declined, with one guest cleaning the laundry themselves.

Value (9/15) — Value concerns prominent with "overpriced" appearing multiple times, one review noting "$55 for unpowered site completely overpriced." Several guests felt accommodations were smaller than advertised, with one stating "cabin was so little for family of 4" and "pictures were shown to what we got was little disappointed." Laundry at $7 per load considered expensive.

Site Size (8/10) — Powered sites described as "spacious" and "very generous for room" with "plenty of room for a larger van." However, "powered sites small" mentioned by some, and unpowered sites criticized as "muddy parking slot right by the pond." Generally adequate but inconsistent across the park.

Sentiment (6/10) — Polarized reviews with many "will definitely be back" but also significant negative experiences. Permanent residents created friction with holiday families, with one detailed complaint about residents being "verbal abusing" and telling kids to "get away." Several families cut stays short due to various issues.

Location (10/10) — Excellent location ratings with "Australia Zoo is only 500 metres down the road" and "4 mins away." Described as "great location" with "courtesy shuttle bus to Australia Zoo," close to Landsborough shops, and "central location to explore Sunshine coast beaches or glass house mountains."

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of children's entertainment infrastructure with no playground, outdated tiny pool, and no kids' activities, making it unsuitable as a holiday destination for families with active children who aren't spending full days at Australia Zoo.

2. Significant conflict between permanent residents and holiday families, with multiple detailed accounts of residents being hostile to visiting children playing, running, or riding scooters, creating an uncomfortable atmosphere that doesn't feel welcoming for family holidays.

3. Inconsistent cleanliness and maintenance with multiple reports of mouldy showers, dirty facilities, deteriorating amenities, broken stove tops, power issues, and a pool desperately needing renovation, suggesting deferred maintenance affecting the guest experience.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"kids loved the ducks and turtles in the pond", "Australia Zoo only 500 metres down the road", "pool super small and no play ground area", "permanent residents ruined this one for us", "no catering whatsoever to kids", "facilities were not clean", "wooded area felt nice to be in natural surrounding", "overpriced for what we got"

Severe cleanliness and maintenance neglect with overgrown grass, broken fences, rotting buildings, septic overflow, and amenities going uncleaned for days despite premium pricing, suggesting management prioritises new cabins over basic camping infrastructure. rationale_honourable: Kenilworth Homestead offers a stunning natural setting on the Mary River with open grassy spaces and beautiful swimming holes that families genuinely love. When attendance is lower during weekdays, the property provides a peaceful retreat with adequate space and helpful staff. While entertainment options like pony rides, flying fox, BMX track, and Easter activities exist, the severely inadequate and poorly maintained amenities significantly undermine what could be an excellent family camping experience.
water_fun: Beautiful Mary River swimming and kayaking

executive_summary:
PARK: Kenilworth Homestead
SCORE: 52/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-05-23
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 79

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (13/20) — Multiple families praised Easter activities including kids disco, flying fox, pony rides, petting zoo with horses/donkeys/alpaca, and BMX track, all included in camping fees with food trucks available. However, some reviews noted activities were poorly run or cancelled without warning, and school camp reviews cited terrible instructor experiences.

Nature (18/20) — Consistently praised for stunning location beside the Mary River with crystal-clear water, beautiful swimming holes, kayaking opportunities, and scenic misty mornings. The natural environment receives universal acclaim with families describing it as "absolutely beautiful," though some note lack of shade and privacy between sites.

Cleanliness (4/15) — This is the park's critical failure point with overwhelming evidence of filthy, inadequate amenities. Recent reviews describe "disgusting" toilets, "grim" showers with hair-clogged drains, septic overflow smells, muddy floors, and facilities going uncleaned for entire 4-day stays. Long-term visitors report significant deterioration over recent years.

Value (6/15) — Mixed feedback with concerns about being "overpriced for the facilities" at $230/night for spa rooms, overcrowding during peak times, and inadequate amenities relative to capacity. Some families appreciate included activities, but value is undermined by facility condition and overbooking issues.

Site Size (8/10) — Generally positive comments about "heaps of room," ability to spread out on the large property, and plenty of grass areas for kids to bike. However, peak periods see families "packed in far too closely" in festival-style camping with insufficient defined spaces.

Sentiment (5/10) — Deeply polarized with loyal repeat visitors of 20-29 years contrasting sharply against recent negative experiences. Multiple families state they won't return until amenities are upgraded, while others cite rude staff and terrible customer service. The sentiment has notably declined in 2024-2026 reviews.

Location (8/10) — Located in scenic Montville/Kenilworth area, close to town with easy access to local bakery and shops. Described as convenient drive from Brisbane with beautiful surrounding countryside, though specific accessibility details are limited.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Catastrophic amenity failure with only 3 showers per gender for 1000+ campers during peak times, leading to 40-minute waits, no hot water, and consistently filthy conditions that multiple long-term visitors describe as the worst deterioration in 20+ years.
2. Severe cleanliness and maintenance neglect with overgrown grass, broken fences, rotting buildings, septic overflow, and amenities going uncleaned for days despite premium pricing, suggesting management prioritises new cabins over basic camping infrastructure.
3. Customer service issues including rude staff interactions, unexplained closures with refusal to refund, inconsistent enforcement of quiet hours allowing noise until 1am, and inadequate supervision creating safety concerns for young children.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"amenities building was in a bad state," "toilets were disgusting over the long weekend," "only 3 showers for the ladies and 3 for the men," "horrifically inadequate for the number of people," "heaps of activities included in the camp fees," "Mary River was beautiful as always to swim in," "buggy man was a really nice friendly guy," "won't be back until amenities are significantly upgraded"

rationale_honourable: Riverside Caravan Park Bli Bli offers a quiet riverfront location with spotless amenities and convenient access to shops across the road, making it suitable for couples and retirees seeking an affordable base to explore the Sunshine Coast. The park features large site spaces and proximity to local attractions like the Aqua Park and beaches, though families must look off-site for children's entertainment.
water_fun: River access only, no pool facilities
kids_play: No on-site playground or kids entertainment

executive_summary:
PARK: Riverside Caravan Park Bli Bli
SCORE: 52/100
DATE SCORED: 2026-04-26
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 58

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (2/20) — Multiple reviews explicitly note the park "doesn't have things like playground, games room or camp kitchen" with families needing to visit nearby attractions like the Aqua Park or park across the river for kids to play.
Nature (14/20) — Strong riverside location with "lovely view of the river" and "great spot near the river," though some complaints about mangrove swamps, dampness, and mosquitoes on certain sites; families can fish at the old boat ramp.
Cleanliness (13/15) — Consistently praised with "spotless amenities," "super clean," and "very clean and tidy," though timed showers frustrate some guests and accessibility concerns noted.
Value (10/15) — Mixed feedback with some finding it "great value" and "inexpensive for the area," while others complained "$42 for an unpowered site is over the top" and "a bit over priced."
Site Size (8/10) — Positive mentions of "great site space (2x normal)," "very large sites plenty of room," though some note sites are "pretty crammed in" with blue metal underfoot.
Sentiment (6/10) — Polarized reviews with loyal repeat visitors ("3rd time back here in 12 mths") versus harsh criticism of management attitude and service recovery during problems.
Location (9/10) — Excellent proximity with "shops galore across the road," "close to Maroochydore, Nambour," and "central to key tourist destinations" making it ideal for exploring the Sunshine Coast.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of family entertainment infrastructure (no playground, games room, camp kitchen, or BBQ facilities) severely limits appeal for families with young children seeking on-site activities.
2. Inconsistent management approach with multiple reviews citing "rude management," poor service recovery during weather emergencies, and confusion over pet policies creating negative experiences.
3. Environmental issues including mosquitoes requiring spray, damp muddy conditions on some non-powered sites near mangroves, and timed showers that caused accessibility problems for guests with disabilities.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"no playground, games room or camp kitchen," "family of 5 - 3 small children," "spotless amenities," "great site space (2x normal)," "Park across the river for kids to play," "close to the Aqua Park," "timed showers," "shops galore across the road," "bring some mozzie spray," "rude management"

rationale_top3: Complete absence of family entertainment facilities - no playground, pool, kids club, or activities for children, making it unsuitable as a family holiday destination. Significantly overpriced for what's offered - multiple reviews cite expensive rates ($50-75/night for powered sites, $225/night cabins) that don't reflect the dated, basic facilities and lack of amenities. Dated infrastructure requiring major updates - facilities described as 'time capsule from the 80s,' with blocked drains, poor lighting, locked camp kitchen, inadequate shower facilities, and maintenance issues throughout.
rationale_honourable: Misleading ocean view claims (20km from coast) and poor site privacy with tight parking arrangements, reducing comfort for families with larger rigs or multiple vehicles.
water_fun: No water facilities mentioned in reviews.
kids_play: No kids entertainment or playground facilities.

executive_summary:
PARK: Ocean View Tourist Park
SCORE: 48/100
DATE SCORED: 2024-01-15
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 34

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (2/20) — No kids entertainment, playground, or recreational facilities mentioned in any reviews. Park appears to have only basic amenities with no organized activities or family entertainment options.
Nature (14/20) — Guests enjoyed views of kookaburras and yellow tail black cockatoos, with mentions of "amazing views" and peaceful surroundings in the Glass House Mountains area. However, the "ocean view" name is misleading ("if you squint you can see water") as it's 20km from the coast. Natural environment is pleasant but limited.
Cleanliness (11/15) — Mixed feedback with many praising "spotlessly clean" amenities and "excellent amenities very clean," but recent reviews note "drains are blocked," "facilities clean, basic and old," and "only a few lights in ladies toilets that work." Maintenance appears inconsistent.
Value (6/15) — Major concern across reviews: "very expensive for basic camp ground," "$156 for 3nights is ludicrous," "$225pn overpriced," "$50 dollar; langs een drukke weg," and multiple complaints that pricing doesn't match the dated facilities offered.
Site Size (6/10) — Several mentions of "bit tight for parking up," "ground small," and guests placed closely together ("put us next to the only three other caravans"). Limited privacy and space for families with multiple vehicles or larger rigs.
Sentiment (5/10) — Divided reviews with positive comments from short-stay guests but significant complaints about value, facilities, and suitability. Several strongly negative reviews ("DOE HET NIET!", "Don't camp here") balanced by those finding it adequate for overnight stays. Limited repeat visitation signals for families.
Location (4/10) — Convenient for Australia Zoo visits ("very close by") and Glass House Mountains exploration, but located "by the busy road" with road noise, 20km from actual ocean, and reception closes at 1pm. Limited nearby family amenities.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of family entertainment facilities - no playground, pool, kids club, or activities for children, making it unsuitable as a family holiday destination.
2. Significantly overpriced for what's offered - multiple reviews cite expensive rates ($50-75/night for powered sites, $225/night cabins) that don't reflect the dated, basic facilities and lack of amenities.
3. Dated infrastructure requiring major updates - facilities described as "time capsule from the 80s," with blocked drains, poor lighting, locked camp kitchen, inadequate shower facilities, and maintenance issues throughout.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"needs a major face-lift", "very expensive for basic camp ground", "facilities clean, basic and old", "close to Australia Zoo", "spotlessly clean", "watching kookaburras, yellow tail black cockatoos", "bit tight for parking up", "ocean view, thin"

executive_summary:
PARK: Mapleton Cabins & Caravan Park
SCORE: 32/100
DATE SCORED: 2025-01-11
REVIEWS ANALYSED: 7

WHY THIS SCORE:
Entertainment (0/20) — No mention of any kids entertainment, playground, activities, games room, or family-focused facilities across all reviews.
Nature (12/20) — Reviews mention "park itself is very beautiful", "quaint little village", and "close to little town, cafes, parks", suggesting pleasant natural surroundings in the Montville hinterland area.
Cleanliness (8/15) — Mixed feedback with multiple mentions of "amenities were very clean" and "amenities clean", but offset by "bathroom facilities very old and basic" and concerns about cleaning duties being "responsibility of long-term residents".
Value (3/15) — Multiple reviews cite poor value with "quite rich for $55 a night", "pricey at $55 per night", "accommodation is overpriced", and one family leaving two days early.
Site Size (3/10) — Sites described as "small, dark, shady and cold", "on gravel", with limited casual sites available; park caters mainly to permanent residents.
Sentiment (3/10) — Highly negative recent reviews from 2024-2025 about dishonest practices, management issues, minimal service, with older 2023 reviews being more balanced; most recent serious warning not to stay.
Location (3/10) — Located near Mapleton/Montville with access to "little town, cafes, parks" but extremely limited office hours (Monday-Friday 11am-4pm only) severely impacts accessibility for families.

WHY NOT 100:
1. Complete absence of family entertainment facilities - no camp kitchen, no playground, no pool, no kids activities making it unsuitable for families with young children.
2. Serious and escalating management concerns with most recent 2024-2025 reviews citing dishonest practices, overcharging attempts, unreturned bonds, and extremely limited office accessibility.
3. Very poor value proposition at $55/night for basic, old amenities with small sites and minimal services, causing guests to leave early and warn others away.

NOTABLE REVIEW PHRASES:
"EXTREMELY DISHONEST, DON'T STAY THERE", "bathroom facilities very old and basic", "no camp kitchen", "quite rich for $55 a night", "small, dark, shady and cold", "left two days early", "office hours seemed to be very minimal", "geared towards permanent or long-term residents"
